subject: [PATCH v3 2/3] drivers/firmware: add SDEI cross-CPU NMI service for arm64

reviews.author: Puranjay Mohan
reviews.summary: Reviewer Puranjay Mohan noted that the initcall for ARM_SDEI_NMI runs unconditionally on arm64 systems without SDEI, causing a warning and an additional error message due to -EIO being returned by sdei_event_register(). He suggested either not shouting about the error or skipping the probe when SDEI is absent.
reviews.raw_body:
This initcall runs unconditionally whenever ARM_SDEI_NMI is built in,
which includes the many arm64 systems that have no SDEI at all. On
those, sdei_event_register() -> sdei_event_create() ->
invoke_sdei_fn() returns -EIO, and the core already complains:
    pr_warn("Failed to create event %u: %d\n", event_num, err);

(that one isn't gated on err != -EIO, unlike sdei_mask_local_cpu() & friends).
We then add a second pr_err() on top, so every boot on a non-SDEI box
with this config gets two alarming lines for what is just "no firmware
support". At minimum, don't shout for -EIO/-EOPNOTSUPP here. Better,
skip the probe when SDEI isn't present  there's no exported predicate
today, but -EIO is the de-facto one.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed to add a check for SDEI presence and gate the initialization of the NMI service on it, indicating that a fix is planned.
reviews.raw_body:
Fair enough. I will add sdei_is_present() and gate sdei_nmi_init() on
it.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-06-15.patch_summary: This patch adds a cross-CPU NMI service for arm64 using the SDEI (System Description Event Infrastructure) software-signalled event mechanism. The goal is to deliver an NMI-like event to an interrupt-masked CPU without the overhead of the pseudo-NMI hot-path. This is achieved by registering a handler for event 0 and signaling it with sdei_event_signal(0, mpidr). The first user of this service is arch_trigger_cpumask_backtrace(), which can now reach masked CPUs. If SDEI is absent or not enabled, the patch falls back to the IPI path. This change also adds a new file drivers/firmware/arm_sdei_nmi.c and updates several other files.
